section: Class 10 Economics - Chapter 2: Sectors of the Indian Economy

notes.summary: The primary sector includes activities that involve extraction of natural resources directly from the earth. It forms the base of all other sectors because it provides raw materials.
notes.importance: Understanding the primary sector helps students recognise the importance of agriculture and natural resources in the economy.

notes.summary: The secondary sector includes activities related to manufacturing and industrial production. It converts raw materials into finished goods.
notes.importance: It shows how industries add value to raw materials and contribute to economic growth.

notes.summary: The tertiary sector provides services that support the primary and secondary sectors. It includes activities that help in distribution and exchange of goods.
notes.importance: Helps students understand the importance of services in modern economies.
quick_facts: Largest contributor to GDP in India

notes.summary: GDP is the total value of final goods and services produced within a country in a year. It is used to measure economic performance.
notes.importance: GDP helps compare economic growth and sector contribution.

notes.summary: Disguised unemployment occurs when more people are working in a job than required, and removing some workers does not affect production.
notes.importance: Common in agriculture and important for understanding employment problems in India.

notes.summary: The organised sector includes registered companies that follow government rules, while the unorganised sector includes small units that often lack job security and benefits.
notes.importance: Helps students understand working conditions and labour rights.
quick_facts: Workers more vulnerable in unorganised sector
